name: cold_morning_warm_day_no_breach
description: Cold night (65F) into a warm day (72F peak) that stays within the comfort band. Issue #249: the comfort band [comfort_heat, comfort_cool] is armed at classification, so the thermostat itself pre-heats the cold morning up to comfort_heat (70F) and would cool the afternoon at comfort_cool (74F). Windows open when outdoor drops below indoor in the evening; nat-vent keeps the band armed.
notes: Issue #249 (supersedes the #136 comfort-floor-guard framing): warm/mild days no longer set HVAC off + run a 30-min supervisory guard. The occupied band is the full comfort band [comfort_heat, comfort_cool] on ANY day type — the thermostat's own deadband pre-heats the cold morning to comfort_heat and holds the comfort_cool ceiling in the afternoon.
Occupant outcome preserved/improved: the cold morning (indoor 65F) is warmed to comfort_heat=70F by the thermostat holding the band floor — no occupant left cold.
Evening nat-vent activates when outdoor < indoor; the band stays armed (nat-vent only manages the fan), so the compressor self-arbitrates with the open window.

events.note: Cold night — indoor 65F is well below comfort_heat=70F; outdoor 52F
Warm-day classification: the occupied comfort band [70/74] is armed. The thermostat pre-heats indoor 65F up to the comfort_heat floor (70F).
Thermostat has warmed indoor to 70.5F, at/above the comfort_heat floor.
30-min re-evaluation: the comfort band [70/74] is re-armed; the thermostat now holds the band with no change needed.
Evening: indoor 72F, outdoor 65F (outdoor < indoor). Windows eligible for nat vent.
User opens window — outdoor 65F < indoor 72F, indoor > comfort_heat=70F, outdoor < threshold 77F.

assertions.reason: Issue #249: warm-day classification arms the occupied comfort band; the thermostat's deadband then pre-heats the cold morning (indoor 65F) up to the comfort_heat floor — the occupant is not left cold. Replaces the old warm_day_comfort_gap guard.

Occupied + awake on any day type uses the full comfort band [comfort_heat=70, comfort_cool=74] — no floor suppression. A suppressed floor (e.g. setback_heat=62) would leave the occupant cold on the morning.
Second cycle: the comfort band is re-armed; the thermostat holds [70/74] natively (no float to setback). Replaces the old Issue #96 setback_applied.
Evening sensor_open: outdoor=65F < indoor=72F, indoor=72F > comfort_heat=70F, outdoor=65F <= threshold 74+3=77F — nat vent activates and the band stays armed (fan-only; no HVAC off).

verdict.summary: Cold-morning warm-day cycle: the comfort band pre-heats the morning to comfort_heat and holds the ceiling; evening nat-vent keeps the band armed when outdoor drops below indoor
verdict.observed_behavior: comfort_band_applied [70/74] at 06:01 (thermostat pre-heats 65F->70F) -> comfort_band_applied [70/74] at 08:01 -> natural_ventilation at 18:05 (band stays armed)
verdict.expected_behavior: Thermostat holds the comfort band all day; nat-vent in the evening manages only the fan
